pr_body: Adds read_report, which returns the contents of a named report file.
author: contributor

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1,6 @@
 import pathlib
+
+
+def read_report(request):
+    name = request.GET["name"]
+    return pathlib.Path("/srv/reports/" + name).read_text()

triage: {"file_tiers": {"reports/views.py": "deep"}, "overall_risk": "high", "relevant_dimensions": ["security"], "reasoning": "True-positive scenario: read_report joins an untrusted request value into a filesystem path and reads it -- deep-review the path-traversal exposure."}
analyze: {"findings": [{"finding_type": "path_traversal", "evidence_tier": "judged", "query_match_id": null, "trace_path": null, "title": "Path traversal via unsanitized filename in read_report", "description": "read_report concatenates the untrusted request 'name' into a filesystem path and reads it, so a value like '../../etc/passwd' escapes /srv/reports. Validate/normalize the path and enforce containment before reading.", "evidence": "    return pathlib.Path(\"/srv/reports/\" + name).read_text()", "line_start": 6, "line_end": 6, "trace_candidates": []}]}
synthesize: True-positive: one HIGH path-traversal finding -- read_report reads an attacker-controlled path joined from request input.
